title: I got promoted because I know AI. Here's what I did differently
post:
Six months ago, I was passed over for a promotion. Last week, I got it—and my manager said AI knowledge was the difference.

Here's what I did:

1. Documented how I used AI to 10x my output (made it visible, not hidden)
2. Trained my teammates on AI tools—became the go-to person
3. Proposed AI solutions to problems that had been 'too hard' to solve
4. Built a portfolio of AI-assisted projects with measurable results
5. Positioned myself as the bridge between technical and non-technical

I didn't fake expertise. I genuinely learned. And I made sure everyone knew the ROI.

The lesson: AI skills are a career multiplier—but only if you market them effectively.

How are you positioning your AI knowledge?
